title: suregada gaultheriifolia
intro: Suregada gaultheriifolia, also known as the bitter wood tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Suregada gaultheriifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. The plant is prepared as a decoction, infusion, or extract, and is often used in herbal preparations to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
